title: 6 Months with AI: How I Went from Mid-Level to Shipping 3x Faster (Real Metrics)
author: Anand Panchal
text:
Member-only story
6 Months with AI: How I Went from Mid-Level to Shipping 3x Faster (Real Metrics)
The unsexy, data-driven truth about AI pair programming
The Baseline: January Me
I was the definition of a solid mid-level developer.
Not struggling, not shining. Just… reliable. I shipped 2–3 pull requests per week. My code reviews came back with 4–5 comments on average. I spent Monday through Wednesday heads-down coding, Thursday fixing review feedback, Friday praying nothing broke in production.
My manager’s feedback in my December review: “Great technical skills. Need to improve delivery speed and take on more architectural work.”
Translation: You’re good, but you’re not growing fast enough.
I made a decision: 2024 would be my AI experiment year. I wouldn’t just use Copilot for autocomplete. I would rebuild my entire workflow around AI assistance — and measure everything.
Here’s what 6 months of data revealed.
If you not have a member on Medium, read from here.
The Metrics I Tracked
I’m a sucker for spreadsheets. I built a Notion dashboard tracking:
